investors: Accel | Lightspeed | Sanjeev Barnwal | Vidit Aatrey | Z47
summary: Fintech startup Oolka raised Rs 130 crore in a Series A round led by Accel India, with participation from Lightspeed, Z47 and individual investors Vidit Aatrey and Sanjeev Barnwal.

STCH, an AI-driven fabric development and textile manufacturing startup founded by former Zetwerk executives, raised $5.5 million in a pre-Series A round led by Omnivore with participation from Kae Capital and WVC.

investors: Blume Ventures | Hardik Pandya | Jasprit Bumrah | MIXI | MS Dhoni | Ravindra Jadeja | Sai Sudharsan | Shreyas Iyer | Tilak Varma | Times Internet | V3 Ventures
summary: LightFury Games raised $11 million in a pre-Series A round backed by Blume, V3 Ventures, MIXI, Times Internet and several Indian cricketers to develop its AAA mobile cricket title eCricket.

investors: Himanshu Singhal | Prakash Govindan | SB Investment (UAE) | Unicorn India Ventures
summary: Deep Algorithm, an AI-powered cybersecurity startup, raised Rs 16 crore in a pre-Series A round led by Unicorn India Ventures, with participation from SB Investment (UAE), Prakash Govindan and Himanshu Singhal.
Spill Games, a Bengaluru-based mobile gaming studio, raised $3.1 million in a seed round co-led by Centre Court Capital and PeerCapital to scale its game portfolio and expand its team.

investors: Blume Ventures | CRED (Dreamplug Technologies) | Peak XV Partners
summary: Consumer AI startup M, co-founded by former Dunzo CEO Kabeer Biswas, is raising Rs 102 crore in a Series Seed round led by Peak XV Partners with participation from Blume Ventures and CRED.

investors: Big Capital | Ramakant Sharma | Redbrook Fund | Roman Saini | Sujeet Kumar
summary: AITS, the parent of Rysen School, raised $4 million in a pre‑Series A round led by Big Capital and Redbrook Fund, with participation from Sujeet Kumar, Ramakant Sharma and Roman Saini, to expand its K‑12 campus network across emerging Indian cities.

investors: Devaiah Bopanna | Himanshu Ratnoo | Kunal Gupta | Roman Saini | Vikrant Potnis
summary: CureMeAbroad, an AI-driven medical tourism discovery platform, raised $600K in a pre‑seed round from five individual investors to expand its technology and growth initiatives.

investors: InfoEdge Ventures | Kae Capital | Susquehanna Venture Capital
summary: GobbleCube,{an AI-driven brand analytics platform}, raised $15 million in a Series A round backed by Susquehanna Venture

investors: ITI Growth Opportunities Fund | Merisis Wealth Trust | PROMAFT Partners | V3 Ventures | family offices
summary: The Hosteller raised Rs 150 crore (approximately $16 million) from a group of investors including PROMAFT Partners and V3 Ventures.

TraqCheck, an HR tech startup, raised $8 million in a Series A round led by IvyCap Ventures with participation from IIFL Fintech Fund to expand its AI-driven background screening and sourcing agents in Europe.

Pluckk, a digital-first fresh food and clean-label FMCG brand, raised Rs 100 crore in a Series A round led by Euro Gulf Investment to expand offline retail, international markets, and AI-driven supply chain capabilities.

investors: A.P. Moller Holding | Advent International | Dragon Funds | Hornbill Capital | Motilal Oswal Alternates | Premji Invest | WhiteOak Capital
summary: Fintech startup KreditBee raised $280 million from a consortium of investors including Motilal Oswal Alternates and Advent International.
Emversity acquired healthcare mobility platform Lanstitut Technologies in a deal valued at around $4.25 million, using cash and stock.
The article reports Bijak's FY25 financial performance, noting a 25% drop in GMV to Rs 551 crore and increased losses of Rs 61 crore, and references its past $20 million Series B round in 2022.
Amazon India announced an investment of Rs 2,800 crore (about $300 million) to expand its logistics and quick commerce infrastructure across the country.
The Indian government announced a Rs 10,000 crore Fund of Funds 2.0 to support deep‑tech, manufacturing and early‑growth startups through segmented AIFs.
Masters’ Union launched a Rs 100 crore early‑stage investment platform, MU Ventures, to fund founders aged under 25 across various sectors.
Bluestone, an omnichannel jewellery retailer, reported a 48% YoY revenue increase to Rs 681 cr in Q4 FY26 and posted a profit of Rs 31 cr.
